metadata.notes: Pre-populated with OWASP Top 10 2021 reference and high-profile npm vulnerabilities. Run /update-security to refresh.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Broken Access Control
owasp_top10_2021.description: Access control enforces policy such that users cannot act outside of their intended permissions.
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Verify package access to filesystem, env vars, and network. Avoid packages that request unnecessary permissions.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Cryptographic Failures
owasp_top10_2021.description: Failures related to cryptography which often leads to exposure of sensitive data.
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Use packages with strong, updated crypto implementations. Avoid deprecated crypto algorithms (MD5, SHA1, DES).

owasp_top10_2021.name: Injection
owasp_top10_2021.description: Injection flaws, such as SQL, NoSQL, OS, and LDAP injection, occur when untrusted data is sent to an interpreter.
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Watch for packages using eval(), new Function(), child_process.exec() with dynamic input.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Insecure Design
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Audit package design and architecture before heavy integration. Prefer packages with security-first design.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Security Misconfiguration
owasp_top10_2021.description: Insecure default configurations, incomplete configurations, open cloud storage, etc.
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Check package default configurations. Disable debug features in production installs.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
owasp_top10_2021.description: Using components with known vulnerabilities or outdated versions.
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: This is the primary concern for npm. Run `npm audit` regularly. Keep dependencies updated.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Verify authentication packages use secure session management and are well-maintained.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
owasp_top10_2021.description: Assumptions related to software updates, critical data, and CI/CD pipelines without verification.
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Use package-lock.json / npm ci for reproducible installs. Verify package integrity hashes. Beware typosquatting.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Ensure installed packages do not suppress error logging or override security-relevant log sinks.

owasp_top10_2021.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
owasp_top10_2021.description: SSRF flaws occur when a web application fetches a remote resource without validating the URL.
owasp_top10_2021.npm_relevance: Packages that make HTTP requests must validate and sanitize URLs. Watch for packages wrapping request/axios without validation.

best_practices: Always use `npm ci` in CI/CD pipelines instead of `npm install`
Pin exact versions in package.json to prevent unexpected upgrades
Prefer packages with >1M weekly downloads and active maintenance
Check package publishing frequency — sudden new publishers can signal takeover
Use `npm pack` to inspect what a package actually ships before installing
Enable 2FA on your npm account to prevent publish-hijacking
Use .npmrc with `audit=true` and `fund=false` for cleaner CI output
Consider using Socket.dev or Snyk for real-time supply chain monitoring
Never run npm install from untrusted scripts or CI configurations
Audit all OAuth apps connected to your Google Workspace every 90 days — revoke apps unused for >90 days
Treat npm/GitHub tokens stored in deployment platform env vars as high-value credentials; rotate every 30–90 days
Mark ALL env vars containing credentials as 'sensitive' in Vercel (opt-in flag) — the default leaves them unencrypted
Use dedicated secrets managers (HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, Doppler) instead of platform env vars for long-lived credentials
After rotating a secret in Vercel, always redeploy every environment that used it — rotation alone does not invalidate existing deployments
Monitor OAuth token usage via Google Workspace Admin audit logs; alert on logins from unexpected IPs or unusual access patterns

vulnerabilities.vulnerability: Prototype Pollution in lodash merge/set/setWith/zipObjectDeep
ReDoS in path-parse
Command Injection in node-ipc (protestware / sabotage)
Sensitive information exposure in axios via SSRF
Prototype pollution in minimist
Malicious package publishes in colors and faker (sabotage)
ReDoS in semver
Path traversal in tar
XSS via prototype pollution in jQuery
Insufficient RegExp complexity control in ws (WebSocket)
SSRF in follow-redirects
Malware in polyfill.io supply chain attack
Backdoor in XZ Utils (liblzma) — supply chain attack
Prototype pollution in micromatch
Supply chain attack in axios — dependency insertion via plain-crypto-js (Sapphire Sleet / DPRK)
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Pin axios to 1.14.0 via package.json overrides. Set ignore-scripts=true in .npmrc.

vulnerabilities.vulnerability: Malicious RAT dropper injected as fake axios dependency (Sapphire Sleet / DPRK)
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Do NOT install this package. Remove immediately. Rotate all secrets/credentials. Set ignore-scripts=true in .npmrc.

vulnerabilities.vulnerability: Path traversal in express static middleware
Supply chain attack in LiteLLM (PyPI) via stolen CI/CD credentials — malicious packages published
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Verify lockfile hashes for litellm installs from late March 2026. Rotate any secrets in CI/CD pipelines that used litellm during this window.

vulnerabilities.vulnerability: Vercel platform breach via compromised OAuth app (Context.ai) — customer env vars exposed including npm/GitHub tokens
vulnerabilities.source: Trend Micro Research / Vercel Security Advisory
vulnerabilities.mitigation: 1) Run `vercel env ls --environment production` and audit all non-sensitive vars. 2) Rotate npm/GitHub tokens, DB credentials, and cloud keys stored in Vercel. 3) Redeploy every environment after rotation. 4) Enable 'sensitive' flag on ALL secret vars.

iocs.initial_vector: Lumma Stealer infection at Context.ai employee

vulnerabilities.vulnerability: OAuth token abuse enabling silent lateral movement across SaaS platforms — bypass of MFA and password rotation
vulnerabilities.version_affected: Any OAuth app with broad Google Workspace scopes
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Audit third-party OAuth apps in Google Workspace Admin Console (Security → API Controls → Third-party app access). Revoke apps unused >90 days. Monitor OAuth token usage for anomalies.
